title: stapelia umbellata
intro: Stapelia umbellata, also known as the "carrion flower," is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Stapelia umbellata include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Stapelia umbellata can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or oil, which can then be used to create medicinal teas, tinctures, or topical creams.
